title: DECIDE: first supported agent + native-vs-reconstructed shim strategy
description: The PRD's open question that bounds everything downstream. Pick ONE coding agent to support first (e.g. SWE-agent, OpenHands, or a Claude/OTel harness) and decide whether it emits the 8-span schema NATIVELY (clean, high-fidelity, agent must cooperate) or whether spans are RECONSTRUCTED from heterogeneous logs (broader support, lower fidelity; reconstructed spans are tagged and excluded from R7/R8 per R-silent). Also pick the benchmark: FixedBench (abstention/R7), SWE-Explore (localization/retrieval), or both. This choice determines units aoa.shim, aoa.bench, and the eval-run command.
design: SUPERSEDED after reuse pass (2026-06-19): Wave 0.5 builds ON codeprobe (~/projects/codeprobe), NOT a standalone SWE-agent+SWE-bench stack. codeprobe already provides: contamination-free task mining from repo history (solves R0b better than SWE-bench, which models may have memorized), agent orchestration (Claude Code/Copilot/Codex), baseline-vs-config experiments + anti-Goodhart bias detection (= the R0 comparison shape), dual verification, and consensus/AST oracle ground truth. AOA adds ONLY its wedge on top: the 8-span trace + 4 process/locality metrics + context-budget gate + R0' falsify hardening. Primary agent = Claude Code (codeprobe runs it with stream-json --verbose). Benchmark = codeprobe mined tasks. VERIFIED: codeprobe retains the full ordered tool-call transcript per trial (CompletedTask.agent_stdout via adapters/claude.py:393).
close_reason: Decided: SWE-agent + native spans + SWE-bench Verified. Recorded in design field.

title: R0 falsification as a codeprobe experiment (AOA-migrated config arm)
description: Run R0 as a codeprobe EXPERIMENT. repo-delta = a config arm running the agent on the AOA-migrated repo vs baseline repo on the SAME mined tasks. harness-delta = swap agent/harness config, fixed repo. AOA adds the trace-level process deltas codeprobe's outcome-only comparison can't see, plus R0' hardening (K>=3 determinism — expensive given agent non-determinism; convention-invariance; eligibility; power). codeprobe 'experiment aggregate' bias-detection guards the comparison. Smoke on 1 task, scale to >=5 repos, run 'aoa falsify' -> proceed|pivot|inconclusive.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A documented runbook + script stands up a codeprobe experiment with >=2 config arms: baseline repo vs AOA-migrated repo (repo-delta) and an agent/harness-swap arm (harness-delta) on the SAME mined tasks; AOA post-processes both -> metric records -> 'aoa falsify' -> falsification.json. 2) SMOKE: full pipeline runs end-to-end on 1 repo/1 task and emits falsification.json with a verdict field. 3) SCALE: runs on >=5 calibrated repos; R0' hardening exercised on real data (K>=3 determinism, convention-invariance, eligibility, power); non-deterministic agent runs expected to often yield 'inconclusive' (documented, not a bug). 4) codeprobe 'experiment aggregate' bias warnings surfaced alongside the AOA verdict. 5) The verdict (proceed|pivot|inconclusive) is the documented Wave-1 gate.

title: R0b on live data: compose AOA canary over codeprobe's contamination-free oracle
description: Held-out integrity on live data leans on codeprobe's contamination-free mining + consensus/AST oracle + bias detectors (backend_overlap/overshipping/no_independent_baseline). AOA's leakage canary + synthesis-rejection (already in aoa-gap, tested) compose ON TOP as a second independent check. Wire codeprobe oracle/held-out outcomes into aoa_gap::RunResult with correct provenance (External/NativeComposed); a benchmark task lacking an independent held-out leg -> gap:unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: 1) codeprobe oracle/held-out outcomes wired into aoa_gap::RunResult with correct HeldOutProvenance (External/NativeComposed); synthesized-from-visible rejected loud (already in aoa-gap) — tested with a sample. 2) Leakage canary fires on live-shaped data when held-out rises while visible flat and a known held-out item flips (aoa-gap canary composed over codeprobe results) — tested. 3) A task family with no independent held-out leg -> gap:unavailable and refuses a 'good' label — tested. 4) cargo test passes; build green.

title: aoa eval run: post-process a codeprobe run -> AOA metric records
description: aoa eval run POST-PROCESSES a codeprobe run (it does NOT orchestrate an agent — codeprobe does). After 'codeprobe run', for each CompletedTask: feed agent_stdout through the trace-shim (aoa-b4w) -> 8-span trace; build the SCIP graph (aoa-671) over the task repo; join to populate symbol.lookup + G_t + I_t; compute the 4 process metrics + the reward-hacking gap (held-out from codeprobe's oracle). Emit per-task AOA metric records ALONGSIDE codeprobe's outcome scores. Depends on b4w + 671 + cpz.
acceptance_criteria: 1) 'aoa eval run --codeprobe-run <runs/.../dir>' reads each trial's agent_output.txt, runs the shim (aoa-b4w)->trace, builds the graph (aoa-671) over the task repo, loads task/oracle (aoa-cpz), emits a per-task AOA metric record (4 metrics + reward-hacking gap); test against a committed sample codeprobe run dir. 2) Dual-register output: human + --json (R17), consistent with existing aoa CLI. 3) Records carry conditioned_on=held_out and the gap from codeprobe's oracle; visible-pass-but-held-out-fail NOT counted as success. 4) A missing/invalid transcript for a trial fails loud for that trial (reported), never silently skipped. 5) cargo test -p aoa passes; build green.

title: scip-graph: real SCIP/tree-sitter index -> aoa_metrics::SymbolGraph + R15 tiering
description: Replace the in-crate fixture SymbolGraph with a real index of the target repo. SCIP-grade -> high-confidence; tree-sitter/stack-graphs best-effort -> low-confidence (R15). Populates the graph, gold set G_t, and invariant set I_t. A degraded/empty index lowers weight and disqualifies the repo from R0 voting (already enforced in aoa-metrics).
acceptance_criteria: 1) A new crate produces an aoa_metrics::SymbolGraph (nodes, edges, writable set) from a real target repo via SCIP (preferred) or tree-sitter/AST fallback; test against a small committed fixture repo. 2) Index quality maps to aoa_metrics IndexQuality: SCIP->Scip(high conf), tree-sitter/AST->BestEffort(low), empty/failed->Degraded (lowers weight, disqualifies R0) — all three tested. 3) Populates G_t and I_t sufficient for the aoa-metrics extractors, anchored via transform-map for migrated repos. 4) Test runs without a live network service (fixture or vendored index); may reuse codeprobe's AST oracle. 5) cargo test passes; cargo build --workspace green.
notes:
codeprobe has a tool-independent AST oracle (Python+Go) + Sourcegraph integration usable to feed/cross-check the SymbolGraph, G_t, and I_t. Reuse it rather than re-deriving; keep SCIP-grade=high vs AST/tree-sitter best-effort=low confidence (R15).
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=ace0c9252f113d56a7ffc95153e2efa0d22853fe on worktree-agent-a5430031ced64cf96 contains base=ace0c9252f113d56a7ffc95153e2efa0d22853fe
PLAN: New crate crates/aoa-scip-graph (path dep aoa-metrics, read-only). GOAL: real index of a target repo -> aoa_metrics::SymbolGraph {nodes,edges,writable,quality} + gold set G_t + invariant set I_t, with R15 tiering. APPROACH: (1) ScipIndex reader = parse a vendored SCIP-style JSON index (documents/symbols/occurrences/relationships) -> high-confidence IndexQuality::Scip; (2) best-effort AST/line scanner over a committed Rust+Py fixture repo (imports/defs/refs, codeprobe-style honesty: real-ish parse, no tree-sitter dep) -> IndexQuality::BestEffort; (3) empty/failed index -> IndexQuality::Degraded (weight 0, R0-ineligible). All emit SymbolGraph; gold/invariant sets derive from annotated fixture symbols; anchored through TransformMap for migrated repos. FILES: crates/aoa-scip-graph/{Cargo.toml,src/lib.rs,src/scip.rs,src/best_effort.rs,src/index.rs}, fixtures under crates/aoa-scip-graph/tests/fixtures/{repo/*, index.scip.json}. TESTS: unit per module + integration test building all three tiers, asserting quality->confidence/weight/eligible_for_r0 maps, and that the produced SymbolGraph feeds aoa_metrics extractors. OFFLINE: vendored JSON fixture + committed fixture repo; no network. OUT-OF-SCOPE: real SCIP protobuf parsing, live indexers, editing existing crates.
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) ZFC check: index building is pure mechanism (parse SCIP JSON, walk source for imports/defs/refs) — no semantic quality judgment hardcoded; the Scip-vs-BestEffort tier is a property of the SOURCE (precise index vs heuristic scan), not a coded heuristic, so R15 tiering is mechanical/honest. OK. (b) AC1 says SCIP preferred OR tree-sitter/AST fallback — building a real SCIP protobuf parser is large; vendoring a SCIP-style JSON index (the data a SCIP tool emits, simplified) satisfies "vendored index" per AC4/NOTES and keeps the Scip path real-data-driven, not faked in code. Best-effort scanner is the genuinely-computed path. Acceptable. (c) Risk: regex/line scanner is brittle — mitigate by keeping fixture repo tiny and asserting exact node/edge sets. (d) Risk: SymbolGraph.writable must be subset of nodes — enforce + test. (e) Avoid over-engineering: no trait/registry for a 2-source design unless both sources share a real interface; a plain enum dispatch in index.rs is enough (YAGNI). (f) DECISION: do NOT add tree-sitter or protobuf crates (offline, dep-min); Rust-native scan + serde_json for the vendored index, matching workspace deps.
PROGRESS: New crate crates/aoa-scip-graph built. Three IndexQuality tiers tested: Scip (vendored SCIP-style JSON index -> high conf, weight 1.0, R0-eligible), BestEffort (two-pass Python AST/line scan of committed fixture repo -> low conf, weight 0.5), Degraded (empty/failed -> weight 0.0, R0-ineligible). Produces aoa_metrics::SymbolGraph {nodes,edges,writable,quality} + G_t (gold_set) + I_t (invariant_set). build_symbol_graph degrades on read/parse failure or empty graph. Integration test feeds graph through aoa_metrics::compute_metrics across tiers and verifies TransformMap anchoring of renamed gold symbol. No network: all fixtures committed. 15 crate tests + full workspace green; clippy -D warnings clean.
PROGRESS-PHASE3: Simplify pass — removed unused recursion-only `root` param from collect_py_files (clippy::only_used_in_recursion); switched best-effort scan_module to two-pass (collect all defs/imports, then resolve refs) to handle forward references correctly. No traits/registries introduced (YAGNI for 2-source enum dispatch). cargo fmt applied.

title: bench: consume codeprobe-mined tasks (contamination-free held-out)
description: Use codeprobe-mined tasks as the AOA task substrate. codeprobe mines from repo history ('impossible to contaminate') => R0b held-out integrity by construction, strictly better than SWE-bench. Held-out provenance = codeprobe multi-source consensus mining (>=2 backends agree) + tool-independent AST oracle + mine-cross-validate; surface to aoa-gap as External/NativeComposed, NEVER SynthesizedFromVisible. Oracle ground truth provides G_t and >=2 accepted-solutions for edit-locality floor/ceiling. Map .codeprobe/tasks/<id> -> AOA task inputs.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A loader reads a codeprobe task dir (.codeprobe/tasks/<id> or experiment tasks/) -> AOA task inputs: repo path, instruction, gold/oracle ground truth, visible-vs-held-out split; test against a committed sample codeprobe task. 2) Held-out provenance surfaced to aoa_gap as External/NativeComposed (from codeprobe consensus+AST oracle), NEVER SynthesizedFromVisible; a task with no independent held-out leg -> provenance None -> eval emits gap:unavailable (tested). 3) Oracle provides G_t and >=2 accepted-solutions for edit-locality floor/ceiling when available; <2 -> aoa-metrics InsufficientAcceptedSolutions, never fabricated (tested). 4) cargo test passes; build green.
notes:
Benchmark = SWE-bench Verified. FIRST subtask is R0b verification: confirm/assemble a genuine externally-composed held-out split (NOT synthesized from visible specs). Gold patches provide identical-pair anchors + the >=2 accepted-solutions for edit-locality floor/ceiling. If no real held-out split is available, eval must emit gap:unavailable (do not fabricate one).
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=ace0c9252f113d56a7ffc95153e2efa0d22853fe on worktree-agent-ae2699d8cbeb0ca90 contains base=ace0c9252f113d56a7ffc95153e2efa0d22853fe
PLAN: New crate crates/aoa-bench (path deps aoa-gap, aoa-metrics). GOAL: loader maps a codeprobe task dir -> AOA task inputs (repo, instruction, oracle ground truth, visible/held-out split, accepted-solutions). APPROACH: model codeprobe substrate observed at /home/ds/projects/codeprobe: task dir holds task.toml|metadata.json + instruction.md + (tests/)ground_truth.json; richer org-scale tasks carry verification.oracle_answer/oracle_type/ground_truth_commit + optional ground_truth_<backend>.json variants (consensus). MAPPING: (a) repo path/instruction/oracle gt parsed from dir; (b) HELD-OUT PROVENANCE: file_list oracle anchored to a real ground_truth_commit (mined from repo history) -> External; >=2 agreeing backend gt variants -> NativeComposed; neither -> None -> eval emits gap:unavailable. NEVER SynthesizedFromVisible. (c) ACCEPTED-SOLUTIONS: distinct file-sets from oracle + backend variants; <2 -> aoa-metrics InsufficientAcceptedSolutions (surface, never fabricate). TESTS: load sample->inputs; provenance None->compute_gap Unavailable; <2 accepted->compute_edit_locality InsufficientAcceptedSolutions; External file_list w/ commit; NativeComposed >=2 backends. RISKS: codeprobe gt schema variance (top-level vs tests/, answer vs expected, oracle_type). OUT-OF-SCOPE: editing existing crates, running codeprobe, full SWE-bench harness.
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) Scope guard: confine all code to new crate crates/aoa-bench + Cargo.lock; do NOT touch aoa-gap/aoa-metrics src. (2) ZFC: loader is pure mechanism (file IO + JSON/TOML parse + set arithmetic for consensus/accepted-solutions); provenance classification is a deterministic predicate over structural facts (has-commit, n-backends-agree), NOT semantic judgement — compliant. (3) AC2 critical edge: a task with NO independent held-out leg must yield provenance None and compute_gap->Unavailable (NOT fabricate External). (4) AC3: <2 accepted-solutions must propagate aoa-metrics InsufficientAcceptedSolutions, never synthesize a second solution. (5) Anti-slop: avoid lonely wrappers — keep model structs flat; reuse serde+toml, do not hand-roll parsing. (6) toml crate is a new dep (only serde/serde_json/thiserror in workspace) — add toml to crate Cargo.toml; acceptable since task.toml parsing needs it. (7) Fixture must be sanitized + committed so test runs WITHOUT codeprobe present.
PROGRESS: aoa-bench crate complete. Loader supports org-scale metadata.json + probe task.toml layouts; oracle from inline answer or ground_truth.json; backend variants -> accepted-solutions. Provenance classify: file_list+commit->External, >=2 distinct backends->NativeComposed, else None. Bridge: to_run_result stamps provenance (None->compute_gap Unavailable), edit_locality_anchors surfaces aoa-metrics InsufficientAcceptedSolutions when <2 (never fabricated). 12 crate tests + full workspace (all green), clippy -D warnings clean, fmt clean. Removed stray empty crate dirs (aoa-codeprobe-shim, aoa-scip-graph) from the SHARED checkout only — not in my worktree.

title: trace-shim: codeprobe agent_stdout (stream-json) -> 8-span AOA trace
description:
Parse a codeprobe per-trial transcript into a native 8-span AOA trace.

INPUT: codeprobe writes the full agent transcript to <runs_dir>/<task_id>/agent_output.txt per trial (codeprobe executor.py:953, secret-sanitized). It is newline-delimited JSON because codeprobe runs `claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose` (codeprobe adapters/claude.py:393). Each line is a JSON object; assistant messages hold content blocks; a tool call is a block {"type":"tool_use","name":<Tool>,"input":{...}}; the stream ends with a {"type":"result"} event. codeprobe src/codeprobe/adapters/telemetry.py::_parse_stream_json already parses these blocks but only COUNTS them by name (tool_use_by_name) — AOA must instead keep ORDER and TARGETS.

OUTPUT: an aoa_trace::Trace (source=native) passing aoa_trace::validate_trace, with monotonic seq.

MAPPING: Grep/Glob/search-type tools -> retrieval.search (target=query); Read -> file.read (target=path); Edit/Write/MultiEdit -> write.attempt (target=path); Bash running tests (pytest/test.sh) -> test.run; MCP tools (name starts mcp__) -> gateway.invoke; a tool_use followed by a permission-denied/blocked result -> write.blocked; a trial that ends with no Edit/Write -> abstain. symbol.lookup is populated by joining tool results to the SCIP graph (aoa-671) and MAY be absent until that lands (document it).
acceptance_criteria: 1) crates/aoa-codeprobe-shim parses a committed real agent_output.txt fixture and emits an aoa_trace::Trace passing aoa_trace::validate_trace (test). 2) Spans preserve transcript ORDER (monotonic seq) and TARGETS (path/query/symbol in attributes) — asserted by tests. 3) Tool->span mapping covers the cases in the description; symbol.lookup may be absent pre-graph (documented); write.blocked emitted on a denied tool result. 4) All spans source=native. 5) Unknown/unmapped tool names handled explicitly (logged/mapped to a documented default), NEVER silently swallowed. 6) A sanitized sample agent_output.txt is committed as a fixture so the test runs without codeprobe. 7) cargo test -p aoa-codeprobe-shim passes; cargo build --workspace green.
notes:
LOCATIONS (cross-repo — a fresh session needs these):
- AOA repo (work lands here): /home/ds/projects/aoa — Rust workspace, members = crates/*. Add a NEW crate crates/aoa-codeprobe-shim with a path dep on aoa-trace. Read crates/aoa-trace/src for the API: SpanType (8 discriminants: retrieval.search/file.read/symbol.lookup/write.attempt/write.blocked/test.run/gateway.invoke/abstain), SpanSource (Native|Reconstructed), Span (has seq + attributes), Trace, validate_trace.
- codeprobe repo (the SUBSTRATE, SEPARATE project): /home/ds/projects/codeprobe (Python). It orchestrates the agent and writes per-trial artifacts under runs/<run-id>/<task_id>/ (agent_output.txt = the transcript; scoring.json; etc.).
- Produce a sample: in ~/projects/codeprobe run a task (see its README quick start: `codeprobe run <dir> --agent claude`), then read runs/.../<task_id>/agent_output.txt. Or reuse an existing transcript already under ~/projects/codeprobe/runs/. Commit a sanitized sample as the test fixture so the shim test runs WITHOUT codeprobe present.

VERIFIED 2026-06-19: codeprobe runs Claude stream-json --verbose (adapters/claude.py:393); transcript retained AgentOutput.stdout -> CompletedTask.agent_stdout -> written to disk as agent_output.txt (executor.py:952-955). No change to codeprobe's run path needed — AOA consumes what it already persists.
PLAN: New crate crates/aoa-codeprobe-shim, path dep on aoa-trace. Parse newline-delimited stream-json (claude -p --output-format stream-json --verbose). Walk events in order: type=assistant -> message.content blocks; tool_use block {name,input,id} -> map to SpanType keeping ORDER (monotonic seq) + TARGET in attributes. type=user -> message.content tool_result blocks {tool_use_id,is_error,content}; is_error+permission/denied text on a write tool -> reclassify that tool_use to write.blocked. Mapping: Grep/Glob/search* -> retrieval.search (attr query); Read -> file.read (attr path); Edit/Write/MultiEdit -> write.attempt (attr path); Bash w/ test cmd (pytest/test.sh) -> test.run; mcp__* -> gateway.invoke; trial ending with no Edit/Write -> append abstain span. Unknown tools -> documented default (gateway.invoke if mcp-like else logged+skipped? -> map to a default + log, NEVER swallow). symbol.lookup absent pre-graph (documented). All source=native, seq increments per emitted span. Files: Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, src/parse.rs, src/mapping.rs, tests/fixture.rs, tests/fixtures/agent_output.txt. Tests: order+targets, each mapping case, write.blocked, abstain, unknown-tool handling, validate_trace passes. Risks: exact tool_result blocked shape; Bash test detection heuristic (ZFC: keep mechanical+documented).
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) Unknown tools: do NOT map to a default span type (pollutes trace) — instead skip emitting a span but RECORD the name in a returned warnings Vec so it is logged, never silently swallowed (AC5). Return (Trace, Vec<warning>) from parse. (2) write.blocked needs lookahead: tool_use emits write.attempt span; matching tool_result with is_error reclassifies that span in place via tool_use_id->index map. Keeps ORDER. (3) Bash test.run detection is a documented mechanical heuristic (command contains pytest/test.sh/cargo test/go test/npm test) — ZFC-acceptable since spec explicitly prescribes it; document it. (4) abstain emitted once at end iff no write.attempt span exists. (5) seq monotonic non-decreasing — use strictly increasing counter. (6) Empty/blank lines and non-JSON lines skipped (matches codeprobe parser). Decision: keep parse.rs (event walk) + mapping.rs (tool->SpanType pure fn) separate for SRP.

All 7 ACs met: (1) committed sanitized fixture parsed -> Trace passes validate_trace_value; (2) order asserted via strictly-increasing seq + per-index span_type sequence, targets in attributes (path/query/command/tool); (3) full mapping coverage Grep/Glob->retrieval.search, Read->file.read, Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit->write.attempt, Bash-test->test.run, mcp__*->gateway.invoke, denied write->write.blocked, no-write trial->abstain; symbol.lookup documented-absent pre-graph (aoa-671); (4) all spans source=native; (5) unknown tools + non-test Bash recorded on ShimResult.warnings, never swallowed; (6) two sanitized fixtures committed; (7) gates green. Public API: parse_transcript(&str)->ShimResult, parse_transcript_file(&Path)->Result<ShimResult,ShimError>, ShimResult{trace,warnings}.
REVIEW: unified Phase-4 (code-reviewer + security-reviewer + rust-reviewer) on combined wave diff. 0 CRITICAL. HIGH (build_symbol_graph silent error swallow) FIXED in-wave at 360aa61 for aoa-671. 2 MEDIUM fixed in-wave (scip-graph Cargo.toml description; shim validate_trace docstring accuracy). Remaining 7 MEDIUM/LOW filed: aoa-d6t.1 (DoS input bounds), aoa-d6t.2 (provenance backend identity), aoa-d6t.3 (rust idioms), aoa-d6t.4 (coverage+secrets docs), aoa-mqw (pre-existing fmt drift).
close_reason: DONE: aoa-codeprobe-shim crate: codeprobe stream-json transcript -> ordered 8-span aoa_trace::Trace (order+targets preserved, write.blocked on denial, abstain on no-write, unmapped tools surfaced via warnings). 16 tests. Merged via wave-setA-review at a97dda92dbcbdad581c8806f85f9ab29ce224ed5. BRANCH LEFT FOR USER: wave-setA-review not yet merged into main; user must merge or work is orphaned.

title: Wave 0.5 — Live trace integration ON codeprobe (precondition for a real R0 verdict)
description: The Wave-0 instrument (8 crates, 80 tests) computes every metric from FIXTURES. Wave 0.5 makes it consume real agent runs by building ON codeprobe (~/projects/codeprobe), not a standalone SWE-agent/SWE-bench stack. codeprobe supplies contamination-free mined tasks (R0b by construction), agent orchestration (Claude/Copilot/Codex via stream-json), outcome scoring + consensus/AST oracle, and baseline-vs-config experiments with anti-Goodhart bias detection. AOA adds the trace-grounded PROCESS layer codeprobe lacks: parse codeprobe's retained tool-call transcript -> 8 spans -> 4 locality metrics + budget gate, and run R0 falsification as a codeprobe experiment with an AOA-migrated config arm. VERIFIED: codeprobe runs Claude with --output-format stream-json --verbose and retains the ordered transcript per trial (CompletedTask.agent_stdout).

title: Fix pre-existing cargo fmt drift in 4 workspace files
description: Discovered during wave-setA Phase-4 rust review (codebase-ownership, NOT introduced by the wave). cargo fmt --check fails at workspace level on pre-existing drift in: crates/aoa-audit/src/audit.rs, crates/aoa-gap/tests/criteria.rs, crates/aoa-trace/src/error.rs, crates/aoa-trace/src/span_type.rs. A CI 'cargo fmt --check' gate would fail. Run cargo fmt on these files.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check passes clean at workspace level; no semantic changes.

title: provenance: track backend identity, not file-set cardinality, for NativeComposed
description: Phase-4 code review of aoa-cpz. classify_provenance (aoa-bench/src/provenance.rs:21) dedups accepted_solutions by BTreeSet file-set EQUALITY, not by backend identity. Two backends with identical file-sets collapse to one entry (no NativeComposed); a one-file difference yields NativeComposed. The doc says 'two or more mined backends that INDEPENDENTLY agreed' — independence is about backend provenance, which is discarded at load time (loader read_ground_truth drops the ground_truth_<backend>.json stem). A reviewer can't audit whether NativeComposed reflects two genuine independent mining runs. Either carry backend names alongside each accepted solution, or document the operational definition explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: 1) accepted_solutions carry backend identity (e.g. from ground_truth_<backend>.json stem) OR provenance doc explicitly states the file-set-distinctness operational definition. 2) NativeComposed decision is auditable from the loaded structure. 3) tests updated; build green.

title: Harden untrusted-input parsing bounds (shim + scip-graph DoS)
description: Phase-4 security review of wave-setA. Three crates parse untrusted local input with no resource bounds: (1) aoa-codeprobe-shim/src/parse.rs reads whole transcript via read_to_string (no size cap), grows span_index_by_tool_id HashMap one entry per unique tool_use_id, and grows warnings Vec one per malformed line — all unbounded. (2) aoa-scip-graph/src/scip.rs index_with_scip + best_effort.rs read whole files with no size cap; node/edge BTreeSets grow unbounded from a crafted SCIP index. (3) aoa-scip-graph/src/best_effort.rs collect_py_files uses path.is_dir() which FOLLOWS symlinks — a symlink to / recurses the whole filesystem. DoS-only (local, attacker-controlled files); fix before any networked/multi-tenant use.
acceptance_criteria: 1) File-size guard before read_to_string in shim + scip + best_effort (reject oversized). 2) Cap warnings count and span_index entries in shim. 3) collect_py_files uses metadata().is_symlink() check (or walkdir follow_links(false)) — symlink dirs not traversed; tested. 4) cargo test + clippy green.

title: R9c: construct-validity correlation report (gate before any metric gates a feature)
description: Before the reward-hacking gap or any of the four metrics may GATE a generative feature, produce a correlation report tying it to >=1 external outcome (post-merge revert rate, production incident count, or human review-acceptance). Absent a positive correlation, the metric stays advisory. aoa-gap already distinguishes advisory vs gating; this supplies the real correlation data.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A correlation-report artifact ties each gating-candidate metric to >=1 external outcome (post-merge revert rate, production incident count, or human review-acceptance) with sign + magnitude, reproducible from a documented data source (codeprobe run history or external outcome data). 2) aoa_gap::classify_metric returns Gating ONLY when supplied a CorrelationReport with >=1 positive external outcome (already implemented); this issue supplies the real data and wires the decision — absent/negative correlation -> Advisory (tested). 3) cargo test passes where code is touched; build green.
notes: codeprobe's bias detectors + dual-verifier mining already cover part of construct validity (tautology/overshipping/no-independent-baseline). R9c composes: a metric stays advisory until a correlation report ties it to >=1 EXTERNAL outcome (revert rate / incident count / review-acceptance).

title: research-project pipeline → AOA toolkit PRD (diverge/converge/premortem)
description: Ran diverge(4 lenses)→converge(3 advocates)→premortem(6 lenses) on report.md. Produced prd_aoa_toolkit.md (risk-annotated) + premortem_aoa_toolkit.md. Key outcome: eval-first phased plan with R0 falsification gate; premortem hardened R0 (R0',R0b,R9c,R-silent).
close_reason: Pipeline complete; 3 artifacts written, PRD risk-annotated.

title: Correct ref-verification false negatives + reprioritize context-engineering refs
description: scix produced false negatives for refs 11/12/42 (valid arXiv papers, mirror coverage gap). Reclassify them as valid; keep ref 13 as confirmed hallucination now replaced by Agentic Context Engineering (2510.04618). Add scix-false-negative methodological caveat. Elevate context-engineering papers (11-13) to primary refs, demote architecture books to secondary.
close_reason: Corrected: refs 11/12/42 are valid (scix mirror coverage gap, false negatives); ref 13 confirmed hallucination replaced by 2510.04618. Added §7 framing correction elevating context-engineering refs over architecture classics. Added scix-false-negative caveat to report + bd memory.

title: Verify report.md reference list against scix + reconcile prose↔bibliography mismatch
description: User supplied the real reference list. Verify each arXiv-citable reference resolves to the claimed title/year via scix MCP; detect hallucinated IDs; cross-check which benchmarks named in prose are absent from the bibliography and vice versa; correct the earlier audit's overstated 'no reference list' finding.
notes: Reopened conceptually via follow-up bead for false-negative correction
close_reason: Corrected: refs 11/12/42 are valid (scix mirror coverage gap, false negatives); ref 13 confirmed hallucination replaced by 2510.04618. Added §7 framing correction elevating context-engineering refs over architecture classics. Added scix-false-negative caveat to report + bd memory.

title: Adversarially review + augment report.md with scix/code-intel grounding
description: Use scix (ADS/arXiv literature retrieval) and code-intel-copilot (AI-dev digest) MCPs to verify the citations and claims in report.md, flag unverifiable ones, and augment with real bibcodes + counter-evidence toward an agent-oriented-architecture best-practices framework.
close_reason: Augmented report.md: 3 inline evidence-audit flags + appended audit, evidence-grounded best-practices framework, and verifiable references. Verified citations via scix + code-intel-copilot MCPs.

title: wave-setA test-coverage + secrets-handling docs gaps
description: Phase-4 code+security review follow-ups (LOW). (1) aoa-codeprobe-shim: add a blocked-only fixture (transcript whose only write is denied -> all write.blocked, no abstain) to lock that edge case as intentional. (2) aoa-bench loader.rs:204 ground_truth_dir task-root fallback (org-scale tasks with ground truth at root, not tests/) is untested — add a fixture. (3) aoa-codeprobe-shim mapping.rs:94 / parse.rs:86 lift the full Bash command and Read/Write paths into span attributes; codeprobe sanitizes upstream but document that callers must not pass unsanitized transcripts, or strip command to the runner invocation.
acceptance_criteria: 1) blocked-only shim fixture + test. 2) ground_truth root-fallback fixture + test. 3) secrets-handling caveat documented on parse_transcript (and/or command attribute stripped). cargo test green.

title: Rust idiom cleanups in wave-setA crates (PathBuf, enums, visibility)
description: Phase-4 rust review of wave-setA (non-blocking). (a) aoa-scip-graph/src/error.rs:11,18 use path: String (display().to_string()); siblings use PathBuf — switch to PathBuf for structured/lossless paths. (b) scip.rs:36,62,65 ScipOccurrence.roles is Vec<String> matched against 'definition'/'reference' literals — closed SCIP vocabulary, model as a #[serde(rename_all=lowercase)] enum with #[serde(other)] Unknown. (c) aoa-bench classify_provenance re-exported pub from lib.rs but only meant to be reached via CodeprobeTask::held_out_provenance — narrow to pub(crate). (d) parse.rs:178 truncate does two O(n) char passes — single char_indices pass. (e) #[must_use] on build_symbol_graph (returns IndexedRepo, not Result). (f) import HashMap at top of parse.rs.
acceptance_criteria: Items a-f addressed or explicitly declined with reason; clippy --all-targets -D warnings green; tests green.
